title: buildah images 'CREATED AT' doesn't report in configured local time
body:
**Description**
The 'CREATED AT' date in 'buildah images' reports in UTC rather than configured local time.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Display current date
```
# date
Tue Mar 27 09:45:46 AEDT 2018
```
2. Compare to time of recently created image in `buildah images`
```
# buildah images
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
95cc7dba2f97         docker.io/library/buildah-ol7:latest                     Mar 26, 2018 22:28     177.4 MB
```

**Describe the results you received:**
'CREATED AT' displays date/time in UTC

**Describe the results you expected:**
'CREATED AT' displays date/time in configured local time

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-0.12-1.git8badcc2.el7.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 0.12 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

comments.body: @pixdrift Thanks for the problem report, we'll take a look at it.
@TomSweeneyRedHat, Curious if it was a conscious decision to use timestamps in buildah rather than relative time like in 'docker images'?
@pixdrif I'll have to let @nalind and @rhatdan handle that answer.  That had been set before I started on the Buildah project.
Not that I know of.  Probably just what @nalind did, would you refer it to be relative like Docker?

Not sure if that was directed at me @rhatdan, I think for consistency with 'docker images' relative format would be a good default for consistency and then make the 'absolute' date as it stands currently an option/parameter that can be specified at the command line?
Not crazy about the options, but I think being consistent with the Docker output makes sense.  We also need to make sure that podman output matches.

The relative ballpark time information always felt unnecessarily vague to me, but I'm not wedded to using absolute timestamps.  Using the local timezone instead of UTC as the default when printing absolute timestamp information makes a lot of sense, though.

-edit-

Too early in the morning, format is ok 😪

Maybe a step towards compliance with the OCI spec v1.0.1 [Properties:Created](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/v1.0.1/config.md#properties) is to use [RFS 3339, Section-5.6](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6) ?

@TomSweeneyRedHat, this is resolved, thanks 👍🍻  much appreciated. I am happy to close this ticket... unless you want to keep it open for discussion on the date format? (which should probably be in a separate issue)
Thanks @pixdrift. I'm happy to further discuss "what the time should be" further, but would like to do so in it's own issue.
